alternative_parents.description: Aromatic heteropolycyclic compounds containing a 6,7-benzomorphan skeleton, which is hydroxylated at the ring 4-position.
alternative_parents.name: 4-hydroxy-6,7-benzomorphans

alternative_parents.description: Heterocyclic Compounds containing a piperidine ring conjugated to a benzyl group through one nitrogen ring atom.
alternative_parents.name: N-benzylpiperidines

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the benzazocine ring system, which consists of a benzene ring bound to an azocine ring.
alternative_parents.name: Benzazocines

alternative_parents.description: Polycyclic aromatic compounds containing a tetralin moiety, which consists of a benzene fused to a cyclohexane.
alternative_parents.name: Tetralins

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a phenylmethtylamine moiety, which consists of a phenyl group substituted by an methanamine.
alternative_parents.name: Phenylmethylamines

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing benzylamine, which consists of a benzene group attached to an amine group.
alternative_parents.name: Benzylamines

alternative_parents.description: Alkylamines in which the alkyl group is substituted at one carbon atom by an aromatic hydrocarbyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Aralkylamines

alternative_parents.description: Phenols that a unsubstituted at the 2-position.
alternative_parents.name: 1-hydroxy-2-unsubstituted benzenoids

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a trialkylamine group, characterized by exactly three alkyl groups bonded to the amino nitrogen.
alternative_parents.name: Trialkylamines

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one nitrogen atom and one carbon atom linked to each other.
alternative_parents.name: Azacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a bond between carbon a pnictogen atom. Pnictogens are p-block element atoms that are in the group 15 of the periodic table.
alternative_parents.name: Organopnictogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a bond between a carbon atom and an oxygen atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organooxygen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic salts that results from the reaction of hydrobromic acid with an organic base.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrobromides

class.description: Opioid alkaloids based on the tricyclic 6,7-benzomorphan skeleton. Benzomorphans are derived from morpholine, but lack its C ring.
class.name: 6,7-benzomorphans

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as 2,6-dimethyl-3-benzazocines. These are aromatic compounds containing a 6,7-benzomorphan skeleton, which is substituted by methyl group at the 2- and 6-positions.

direct_parent.description: Aromatic compounds containing a 6,7-benzomorphan skeleton, which is substituted by methyl group at the 2- and 6-positions.
direct_parent.name: 2,6-dimethyl-3-benzazocines

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Aromatic compounds containing a 6,7-benzomorphan skeleton, which is substituted by methyl group at the 2- and 6-positions.
subclass.name: 2,6-dimethyl-3-benzazocines

superclass.description: Naturally occurring chemical compounds that contain mostly basic nitrogen atoms. This group also includes some related compounds with neutral and even weakly acidic properties. Also some synthetic compounds of similar structure are attributed to alkaloids. In addition to carbon, hydrogen and nitrogen, alkaloids may also contain oxygen, sulfur and more rarely other elements such as chlorine, bromine, and phosphorus.
superclass.name: Alkaloids and derivatives
